name: Web App Rewrite
description: Full web app rewrite with 4 agents, 8 tasks in a linear dependency chain, demonstrating blocking, escalations, reservations, messaging, and export/import

modules.name: api-gateway
modules.summary: HTTP routing layer — Express.js with rate limiting and request validation

modules.name: auth-service
modules.summary: JWT authentication + session management with Redis backing

modules.name: data-layer
modules.summary: PostgreSQL models, migrations, and query layer (Prisma)

modules.name: frontend
modules.summary: React 18 SPA with TypeScript, React Query, and Tailwind CSS

agents.name: alice
agents.summary: Senior backend engineer — 5yr exp, owns auth and API

agents.name: bob
agents.summary: Frontend specialist — React expert, accessibility focus

agents.name: carol
agents.summary: Staff engineer — code reviewer, architecture guardian

agents.name: dave
agents.summary: Tech lead — planner, architect, tie-breaker on decisions

tasks.name: design-architecture
tasks.summary: Design system architecture: service boundaries, API contracts, data model

tasks.name: implement-auth
tasks.summary: OAuth2 + JWT: login/logout, token refresh, session management, Google provider

tasks.name: implement-api
tasks.summary: REST API endpoints: /users, /sessions, /auth/*, rate limiting, validation middleware

tasks.name: implement-frontend
tasks.summary: React SPA: login page, dashboard, user profile, API integration via React Query

tasks.name: integration-tests
tasks.summary: End-to-end test suite: auth flow, CRUD operations, error scenarios, load testing

tasks.name: code-review
tasks.summary: Full codebase review: security audit, performance review, accessibility check

tasks.name: deploy-staging
tasks.summary: Deploy to staging: Docker compose, CI/CD pipeline, smoke tests, monitoring setup

docs.name: api-spec
docs.summary: REST API specification: endpoints, request/response schemas, auth headers, error codes

docs.name: auth-design
docs.summary: Auth architecture: OAuth2 flow diagrams, JWT structure, session lifecycle, security considerations

plans.name: rewrite-plan
plans.summary: Web App Rewrite v2 — master plan covering 8 tasks across 4 modules

cycles.title: Planner completes architecture design
cycles.scene: Dave the architect kicks off the project by designing the system.

actions.body: Architecture finalized. DB schema in api-spec doc. Start with PostgreSQL setup — tables: users, sessions, oauth_tokens. Use UUID PKs.
Frontend can use React Query for API calls. See api-spec for endpoints. Auth will use httpOnly cookies, not localStorage.

cycles.title: Database setup with file reservations
cycles.scene: Alice claims the database work and locks the relevant files.

actions.body: DB migrations ready for review: 0001_users.sql, 0002_sessions.sql, 0003_oauth_tokens.sql. Indexes on email and session_token.

cycles.title: Blocker escalation
cycles.scene: Alice starts auth but hits a wall — she needs OAuth credentials nobody provided.

actions.body: BLOCKED: Need OAuth2 client_id and client_secret for Google provider. Cannot proceed without credentials. Who has access to the Google Cloud Console?

cycles.title: Question escalation
cycles.scene: While blocked, Alice raises a design question too.

actions.body: Should we support both OAuth2 and SAML, or just OAuth2? SAML adds ~2 days of work but enterprise customers need it. What's the priority?

cycles.title: User resolves escalations, work continues
cycles.scene: The user responds to Alice, unblocking her.

actions.body: OAuth2 only for now. We'll add SAML in v2. Credentials: use env vars OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET — they're in the team vault.
Auth module complete. JWT tokens with 1h expiry, refresh token rotation every 7d. Google OAuth2 flow tested. Ready for review.

cycles.title: File reservation conflict
cycles.scene: Alice and Bob both try to claim the API directory.

cycles.title: Parallel work + inter-agent messaging
cycles.scene: Alice builds the API while Carol reviews the auth work. Agents communicate directly.

actions.body: Auth review complete: LGTM overall. One nit: add rate limiting to /auth/token endpoint (10 req/min per IP). Non-blocking, can be done during API work.
Auth module review complete. Code quality is solid. JWT implementation follows OWASP guidelines. Recommending we proceed to API phase.

cycles.title: Frontend work + failure scenario
cycles.scene: Bob starts the frontend but hits a React 19 breaking change.

actions.body: BLOCKED: Build fails on React 19. Breaking change in useEffect cleanup causes 12 component failures. Options: (1) downgrade to React 18 (~30min), (2) refactor all 12 components (~4hrs). Need a decision.
Downgrade to React 18 for now. We'll upgrade post-launch when the ecosystem stabilizes.
Frontend complete. React 18, TypeScript strict mode, 94% Lighthouse score. React Query handles all API state. Ready for review.

cycles.title: Context queries and graph exploration
cycles.scene: Step back and explore the knowledge graph we've built.

cycles.title: Final sprint — close remaining tasks
cycles.scene: The team sprints to finish. Integration tests, code review, then deploy.

actions.body: Full review complete. 3 minor issues filed, no blockers. Security audit passed. Ready for staging deploy.

cycles.title: Export/import round-trip
cycles.scene: Export everything, import into a fresh project, verify integrity.
